_meta.description: Drill Class x Tier matrix. Class and Tier are INDEPENDENT — any class can be any tier. Upgrade Templates raise tier and fully restore durability. Legendary is the ceiling. No movement weight penalty.
_meta.status: FIRST-PASS BALANCED (2026-07-05) — dig_time_mult and durability set on a deliberate dramatic tier curve (Common→Rare→Epic→Legendary big power leaps, Legendary a huge windfall); spawn_weights sum to 100. Values are testable first-pass, NOT final; pending playtesting.
_meta._balanced: 2026-07-05 first-pass balance pass — testable values pending playtesting
_meta._balance_note: Precision/Thermal/Resonance share one base curve; Burst is intentionally slightly slower and less durable per tier because it destroys 2 tiles per dig. Tier jumps are dramatic by design.

fields.dig_time_mult: multiplier on base terrain dig time (lower = faster). Per-terrain class strength lives in terrain_stats.json class_effectiveness.
fields.durability: number of tile-breaks before the drill is consumed/needs an Upgrade Template
fields.spawn_weight: relative class spawn weight out of 100; Precision most common, Resonance rarest

_meta._note_spawn_weight: Drill class spawn rates live HERE (spawn_weight per class), not in spawn_rates.json — DrillClassData.spawn_weight() reads this file. Weights sum to 100.

Burst.role: TBD — area/multi-tile digging (destroys 2 tiles per dig, so slightly slower and less durable)

Thermal.role: TBD — heat-based; excels vs soft/organic terrain. NOTE: dig calc currently ignores class_effectiveness for Thermal (uniform speed), so this strength is intent-only for now.

Resonance.role: TBD — vibration-based; balanced generalist across all terrain (never best, never worst).
